title: Database Query Simulator
description: An interactive SQL query sandbox with a custom regex-based lexical tokenizer, relational join evaluator (Cartesian product + ON equality), WHERE filter supporting =/></like operators, and live tabular output rendering — all in native JavaScript with zero dependencies.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Custom SQL lexer: tokenize() splits user input into typed tokens (keyword, ident, string, number, op, comma, star, dot, parens) using character-level scanning with quote-aware string capture

Recursive descent parser: parse() builds AST with SELECT columns (star or list with optional table.column notation), FROM source table, INNER JOIN … ON left.col = right.col, and WHERE clauses with AND chaining
Relational join executor: Cartesian product of source × joined table rows, filtered by ON equality comparison (String(leftVal) === String(rightVal)) for matching foreign keys
WHERE filter: row-level evaluation supporting = (string equality), > and < (numeric comparison), and LIKE (regex-converted from % wildcards) with AND compounding
Error handling: unknown table detection, missing keyword guidance, undefined column resolution via resolveCol() with table hint disambiguation — all displayed as crimson overlay messages
Tabular output: DOM-generated table with .otHead header row and .otRow data rows, column keys extracted from Object.keys() of first result row, alternating row shading
Preset templates: 6 macro queries via dropdown (Select All Users, Filter Orders by Value, Join Users+Orders, Users from USA, Shipped Orders, Admin Emails) loadable with one click
Telemetry panel: execution latency (performance.now), record count, query phase state machine (idle → parsing → executing → complete/error), and status indicator with color-coded CSS classes
